zh.lifeSummary: 當John I Duke of Lorraine於1346年1月9日出生在法國洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希時，他的父親Rudolph Duke of Lorraine26歲，母親Marie de Chatillion-Blois23歲。他於1361年12月16日與Sophie von Württemberg在德國巴登-爾特波格斯圖特加特結婚。他們至少是2個兒子和2個女兒的父母。他去世於1390年9月23日在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年44歲，埋葬在法國洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John I Duke of Lorraine于1346年1月9日出生在法国洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希时，他的父亲Rudolph Duke of Lorraine26岁，母亲Marie de Chatillion-Blois23岁。他于1361年12月16日与Sophie von Württemberg在德国巴登-爾特波格斯圖特加特结婚。他们至少是2个儿子和2个女儿的父母。他去世于1390年9月23日在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲享年44岁，埋葬在法国洛萊姆特-吉莫瑟勒南希。

nameMeanings.name: Duke
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English duk(ke), duck, doke, dook ‘duck’ (Old English dūce), either from a perceived resemblance (perhaps a waddling gait) or from association with wild fowling. Compare Duck , Drake .

English: from the Middle English personal name Duk or Duke. In northern England this is usually a pet form of Marmaduke. It may alternatively be a survival of one or more Old English personal names, though it is uncertain whether they were still current in the period of surname formation. Old English Ducc(a) is attested in placenames like Duxford (Cambridgeshire) and Duckington (Cheshire), and was perhaps interchangeable with Docc, attested in Doxey (Staffordshire) and Doxford (Northumberland). Duke could also represent Old English Deowuc (as in Deuxhill, Shropshire). A surname from Marmaduke is on record until at least 1881 and derives from the personal name Marmaduke, apparently an Anglo-Norman French pronunciation of Old Irish Maolmaedóc ‘devotee of Maedóc’; see Duckett .
Americanized form of Polish Duk: nickname from dukać ‘to stammer or falter’.

variants.name: Dock
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Duke  .
Norwegian: habitational name from a farm named Dokk, from Old Norse dǫkk ‘hollow, depression’.
North German (Lower Saxony): possibly a metonymic occupational name for someone who worked in the cloth trade, from Middle Low German dōk ‘fabric’.

variants.name: Doke
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .

variants.name: Duch
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Nga, Phal, Phan, Sophan, Tha, Thang, Yen.  German Kurt, Klaus.
Polish, Sorbian, and Ukrainian: nickname from duch ‘spirit, breath’.
Czech: from a short form of the Old Czech personal name Duchoslav, composed of the elements duch ‘spirit’ + slav ‘glory’ or ‘famous’.

variants.name: Duckett
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Duket. This may sometimes have been an early Middle English pet form of an Old English Ducc(a), but was more often a pet form of Marmaduke, especially in Yorkshire (see Duke ). Marmaduke itself is from Irish Maol Maedóc ‘servant of Maedóc’, Maedóc being the name of prominent Irish saints of the 6th and 7th centuries.

variants.name: Duck
meanings: English: variant of   Duke  .
Irish: adoption of the English name (see 1 above) as an equivalent of Lohan (an Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Leocháin ‘descendant of Leochán’) by mistranslation, as if from lacha ‘duck’.
North German (also Dück) and German (Dück): see Dueck .

variants.name: Dukes
meanings: English: variant of Duke , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Kyong, Kyong Suk, Young, Chae, Chi Young, Hang, Kil, Rin, Sang Hun, Chang, Chong, Insoo, Kum, Sun Young, Yom, Young Hwan.  Cambodian Kiwan, Oeurn, Phoeun, Pich, Saroeuth, Seung, Sophath, Sophea, Yoeun, Phan, Son.  Indian Shanti, Harendra.
Indian (Uttar Pradesh): Brahmin name, from Sanskrit om, the Hindu sacred and mystic syllable uttered at the beginning of prayers.
Cambodian: written ឱម, corresponding to the exclamation of solemn affirmation, of Sanskrit origin (see 1 above), chanted by Buddhist monks.

variants.name: Oum
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Heang, Hye, Kam, Kan, Kheng, Khin, Kun, Sang, Sok, Phong, Yom, Yoo, Youn, Cuong, Phal, Samnang, Tin.  Cambodian Sokha, Chheang, Choeun, Moeun, Roeun, Sambath, Samoeun, Savuth, Soeun, Sopheap.
Cambodian: written អ៊ុំ, from a Khmer word denoting an elder sibling of one's parent (i.e. ‘uncle’ or ‘aunt’). Compare   Om   3 and   Um   2.

nameMeanings.name: Lorraine
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Emile, Perrine, Pierre.

French and English: habitational name from Lorraine, a region in the northeastern part of France. Its name derives from the name of the medieval kingdom of Lotharingia, which in turn was named for its sovereign Lothar (a personal name composed of the elements hlūd ‘famous, renowned’ + hari, heri ‘army’).
Altered form of French   Lorrain  , a variant of 1 above.

variants.name: Loring
meanings: English: ethnic name from Old French Lohereng ‘man fromLorraine’ (see Lorraine ).

variants.name: Lorrain
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Adrien, Alban, Andre, Desire, Fernande, Philippe, Pierre.
French: variant of   Lorraine  . This surname is also found in Britain. Compare   Laurain   and   Laurin  .
History: Pierre Lorrain dit Lachapelle from Angliers in Vienne, France, married Françoise Hulin in France c. 1656.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   John  , also used quite frequently in the English-speaking world. In Northern Ireland it is sometimes found as a variant of   Ewan .

en.lifeSummary: When John I Duke of Lorraine was born on 9 January 1346, in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France, his father, Rudolph Duke of Lorraine, was 26 and his mother, Marie de Chatillion-Blois, was 23. He married Sophie von Württemberg on 16 December 1361, in Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 sons and 2 daughters. He died on 23 September 1390, in Paris, Île-de-France, France, at the age of 44, and was buried in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John I Duke of Lorraine est né le 9 janvier 1346, à Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France, son père, Rudolph Duke of Lorraine, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Marie de Chatillion-Blois, avait 23 ans. Il a épousé Sophie von Württemberg le 16 décembre 1361, à Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils et 2 filles. Il est décédé le 23 septembre 1390, à Paris, Île-de-France, France, à l’âge de 44 ans, et a été enterré à Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als John I Duke of Lorraine am 9. Januar 1346 in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lothringen, Frankreich geboren wurde, war sein Vater Rudolph Duke of Lorraine 26 und seine Mutter Marie de Chatillion-Blois 23. Er hat am 16. Dezember 1361 in Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Deutschland Sophie von Württemberg geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Er starb am 23. September 1390 im Alter von 44 Jahren in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich und wurde in Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lothringen, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John I Duke of Lorraine è nato il il 9 gennaio 1346 nel Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia, il suo padre, Rudolph Duke of Lorraine, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Marie de Chatillion-Blois, aveva 23 anni. Lui sposato Sophie von Württemberg il 16 dicembre 1361, nel Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 23 settembre 1390, nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, all'età di 44 anni, e fu sepolto a Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: John I Duke of Lorraineが1346年1月9日にフランスロレィヌメウルスエ-エト-モセルナンシで生まれた時，父Rudolph Duke of Lorraineは26歳，母Marie de Chatillion-Bloisは23歳であった。1361年12月16日にドイツバデン-ウuルッテンバーグストゥットガートでSophie von Württembergと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼は1390年9月23日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスで44歳で死去し，フランスロレィヌメウルスエ-エト-モセルナンシにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John I Duke of Lorraine(이)가 1346년 1월 9일 프랑스 로레이인 우르테-트-모셀 난시에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Rudolph Duke of Lorraine(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Marie de Chatillion-Blois(은)는 23세였다. 그는 1361년 12월 16일 독일 바데-wu르테버그 스투트가트에서 Sophie von Württemberg(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1390년 9월 23일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 44세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 로레이인 우르테-트-모셀 난시에 묻혔다.

mn.9 January 1346–30 September 1390: 1346 оны Нэгдүгээр сарын 9–1390 оны Есдүгээр сарын 23

pt.9 January 1346–30 September 1390: 9 de janeiro de 1346–23 de setembro de 1390
pt.lifeSummary: Quando John I Duke of Lorraine nasceu em 9 de janeiro de 1346, em Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, França, seu pai, Rudolph Duke of Lorraine, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Marie de Chatillion-Blois, tinha 23 anos. Ele casou-se com Sophie von Württemberg em 16 de dezembro de 1361, em Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Alemanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos e 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 23 de setembro de 1390, em Paris, Île-de-France, França, com 44 anos, e foi sepultado em Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John I Duke of Lorraine родился 9 января 1346 г. в местности нанси, меурте-ет-моселл, лорреаин, Франция, его отцу по имени Rudolph Duke of Lorraine было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Marie de Chatillion-Blois, 23. Он женился на женщине по имени Sophie von Württemberg 16 декабря 1361 г. в местности стуттгарт, баден-вuрттембирг, Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына и 2 дочери. Он умер 23 сентября 1390 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция в возрасте 44 лет и был похоронен в местности нанси, меурте-ет-моселл, лорреаин, Франция.

es.9 January 1346–30 September 1390: 9 de enero de 1346–23 de septiembre de 1390
es.lifeSummary: Cuando John I Duke of Lorraine nació el 9 de enero de 1346, en Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia, su papá, Rudolph Duke of Lorraine, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Marie de Chatillion-Blois, tenía 23 años. Se casó con Sophie von Württemberg el 16 de diciembre de 1361, en Stuttgart, Baden-Württemberg, Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 23 de septiembre de 1390, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia, a la edad de 44 años, y fue sepultado en Nancy, Meurthe-et-Moselle, Lorraine, Francia.
